latest.title: Form 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI)
latest.content:
29 Apr 2026 08:43
RNS Number : 3848C
Cavendish Securities PLC
29 April 2026
FORM 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI)
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN
EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITHOUT RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY ("RI") STATUS (OR WHERE RI STATUS IS NOT APPLICABLE)
Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Name of exempt principal trader:
Cavendish Securities Plc
(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(d) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
28/04/2026
(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
N/A
2. POSITIONS OF THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
ORD 0.5P
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
-357
-0.01
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
-357
-0.01
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchases/ sales
Total number of securities
Highest price per unit paid/received
Lowest price per unit paid/received
ORD 0.5P
ORD 0.5P
Sales
Purchases
400
2,359
635
625
635
621.24
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO
Date of disclosure:
29/04/2026
Contact name:
David Evans
Telephone number:
+44 (0) 20 7220 0599
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Audioboom Group PLC runs a cloud‑based platform that lets podcasters, radio stations and brands create, host, distribute and monetize audio content.  The service handles everything from recording tools to streaming and analytics, turning raw audio into a revenue‑generating asset.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Podcasting is a fast‑growing media segment, now worth billions in advertising and subscription revenue.  Audioboom’s platform sits at the heart of that ecosystem, giving creators a single place to manage distribution across all major podcast networks and streaming services.  Its reach and technology give it a moat against smaller hosting providers.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been in a period of heavy cash burn, with the latest quarterly reports showing continued losses and a need for fresh capital.  Management has been re‑examining its product mix and looking for strategic partners or a takeover to stabilize the business.  The stock is trading at a negligible price, reflecting a deep loss of shareholder confidence and the risk of further dilution.

rns_narrative: Audioboom has released a series of updates that focus on its financial health and a strategic review of its core business.  The latest filings highlight a push to reduce costs, streamline operations, and explore potential partnerships or a sale to a larger media player.

The company is also addressing governance matters, with board changes and a renewed focus on executive compensation and shareholder rights.  These moves suggest an attempt to restore confidence and prepare for a possible turnaround.
The narrative points to a company that is still struggling to find a profitable path but is actively seeking external capital or a strategic exit to survive.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent speculation about a takeover or fresh funding
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders believe a large media firm may step in with a premium bid, citing the platform’s strong user base and the industry’s consolidation trend.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the company’s cash burn is unsustainable and that any takeover would likely come with a steep discount to current share prices, leaving existing shareholders with little value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has shifted from generic commentary to focused speculation about a takeover, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no search activity for Audioboom, indicating that the general public is unaware of the company’s situation—an ideal setup for a contrarian play if a catalyst emerges.

near_term_catalysts: The next earnings release in May 2026 could provide a clear picture of whether the cost‑cutting plan is working or whether a fresh capital raise is required.
A formal takeover offer from a larger media conglomerate would instantly lift the share price and provide a realistic exit for investors.
Any announcement of a new partnership or product launch that expands the platform’s reach could signal a pivot toward higher revenue streams.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a break above the 50‑day moving average, or a clear statement from management about a potential sale or new funding round.  A spike in institutional buying would also be a strong confirmation of a bullish reversal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle; it has not yet hit a clear low and is still trading far below its historical highs.  The APEX score (if high) would confirm a tight compression that could turn into a breakout once a catalyst hits.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – there is no concrete signal yet, and the risk of further decline remains high.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If a takeover or fresh capital injection occurs, the share could jump 2–3 times its current price.  Given the depth of the crash, a bounce to the mid‑range of the 52‑week band would deliver a 200–300% return on the price paid.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a new funding source or a strategic partner, the company may continue to burn cash and lose market share.  The worst case is a continued slide to zero or a forced liquidation, wiping out all equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst appears, but the probability of that event is low.  The risk is essentially the entire current market value, while the upside could be 3–5 times that if the company turns around.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only if the share price climbs to a strong support level (e.g., the 20‑day moving average) and volume spikes by at least 50% over the average.  Size positions to risk no more than 2–3% of capital per trade and set a stop 10% below the entry point.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement of a takeover bid or a new funding round.  Monitor institutional activity and any change in the company’s debt profile.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or files for bankruptcy, exit immediately.  No position should be held if the company’s liquidity position deteriorates further.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Audioboom is in a deep slump with no clear catalyst; the risk of continued decline outweighs the potential upside until a takeover or fresh funding materialises.
